description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, utilitarian fuselage with a matte olive-green finish, typical of military or utility helicopters. The main rotor consists of two long, straight blades mounted atop a central mast, while the tail boom extends rearward to a tail rotor with a protective frame. The cockpit is equipped with large, angular windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for the pilots. The landing gear consists of two sturdy, tubular skids attached to the underside of the fuselage with crossbars for stability. The surface of the helicopter appears weathered, with visible wear and subtle panel lines, suggesting frequent use. No visible registration numbers or distinct markings are present on the exterior.
description: A weathered olive-green helicopter with large cockpit windows, two main rotor blades, a tail rotor, and tubular landing skids. The fuselage is utilitarian and shows signs of frequent use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide cockpit windows, central rotor mast, and symmetrical landing skids.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, main rotor, tail boom, and the left landing skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit profile, right landing skid, and tail rotor structure.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades made of composite or metal, dark gray in color, with no visible markings. Each blade spans nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: The tail rotor is mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, featuring two blades with a protective frame. The blades are gray and appear to be made of metal or composite material.
attribute_landing_skids: The landing skids are tubular, metallic, and painted gray, with crossbars connecting them for added stability. They are simple in design and show signs of wear.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit has four large, angular windows: two front-facing and two side windows, all clear with a slight tint and framed by metal. The windows provide wide visibility and are bordered by visible seams.
